macro_segments.title: The Mishnah's Case: Three Brothers, Two Sisters, One Unrelated Woman, and the Conditions for Remarriage

micro_segments.title: The Basic Scenario: Reuven and Shimon Married to Rachel and Leah; Levi Married to Devorah
Stage One: Divorce of Leah; Stage Two: Death of Levi; Stage Three: Shimon Marries Devorah
The Resolution: When Leah is Out of the Picture First, Devorah is Permitted to Reuven After Shimon Dies

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Question: Does the Mishnah Assume Yesh Zika or Ein Zika?

micro_segments.title: The Extreme Case in the Mishnah: Leah Divorced Before Levi Dies, Avoiding Any Zika Problem
The Middle Case Not in the Mishnah: Levi Dies First, Then Leah is Divorced—Does Zika Create a Problem?
Rav Ashi's Position: Yesh Zika Even with Two Brothers; Rav Nachman's Contrary View: Ein Zika Even with One Brother
Rav Nachman's Source for Ein Zika: The Earlier Mishnah Requiring Ma'amar, Not Just Zika, to Create a Problem
Rav Ashi's Response: The Ma'amar in That Mishnah Merely Excludes Beit Shammai's View, Not That Zika Alone Suffices

macro_segments.title: Reading the Mishnah's Case Against Ein Zika or Yesh Zika: What Does the Mishnah Exclude?

micro_segments.title: According to Rav Nachman (Ein Zika): The Mishnah Excludes the Case of Shimon Marrying Devorah Then Divorcing Leah
The Debate Over Nishuin Rishonim (Marriage Determines Status) vs. Mita Mappelet (Death Determines Status)
According to Rava (Mita Mappelet): The Mishnah Excludes the Case Where Levi Dies but Leah Was Never Divorced
Rava's Conclusion: The Mishnah Must Hold Yesh Zika (Supporting Rav Ashi) and Nishuin Rishonim

macro_segments.title: The Second Mishnah: Safek Kiddushin and Safek Gerushin—When the Status of an Erva is Uncertain

micro_segments.title: The Principle: When an Erva's Status is Uncertain (Married or Not), the Co-wife Must Do Chalitza, Not Yibbum
Safek Kiddushin: The Ring Fell Between Them and We Don't Know If She Acquired Kiddushin
Safek Gerushin: Three Types of Defective Gets (Handwriting Without Witnesses, Witnesses Without Date, Date Without Two Witnesses)
Why the Mishnah Did Not Give the Parallel Case of a Rolled-Up Get as Safek Gerushin

macro_segments.title: Rava's Principle: Chazakah (Status Quo) Determines Status in Cases of Safek; The Case of Safek Get

micro_segments.title: Rava's Logic: A Woman's Status Before the Safek Remains Her Status; Do Not Change It Based on Doubt
Safek Get Does Not Change Rachel's Status (Still Married) Nor Devorah's Status (Still Permitted to Yibbum)
The Gemara's Challenge: Why Not Apply the Same Chazakah to Safek Kiddushin?
The Gemara's Answer: Safek Kiddushin Requires Chalitza Because It Is a Serious Chumra (Stringency) Not Leading to Leniency

macro_segments.title: The Cost-Benefit Analysis of Stringencies: When a Chumra Is a No-Cost Stringency vs. When It Leads to Negative Consequences

micro_segments.title: Safek Kiddushin as a No-Cost Chumra: Chalitza Sends the Correct Message (Rachel Out of Picture) and Yibbum Would Be Permitted Anyway
The Gemara's Counterargument: Why Not Require Chalitza for Safek Get as Well?
Chalitza for Safek Get Would Send the Wrong Message: It Implies Rachel Is Divorced When Status Quo Says She Remains Married
The Solution: Go By Status Quo for Safek Get; Do Not Require Chalitza Because It Would Lead to Yibbum with a Real Erva

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Challenge: The Case of a Building Falling on Both Shimon and His Niece—Uncertainty About Who Died First

micro_segments.title: The Setup: Shimon is Married to Rachel (Reuven's Daughter, an Erva to Reuven) and to Devorah; Building Falls on Both Shimon and Rachel
The Problem: If Rachel Died First, Devorah Is Permitted to Yibbum; If Shimon Died First, Devorah Is Forbidden (Tzarat Erva)
The Mishnah's Solution: In Safek, Devorah Does Chalitza, Not Yibbum
Abaye's Application of Rava's Logic: Why Not Let Devorah Retain Her Pre-Safek Status Quo (Permitted to Yibbum)?
The Difference from Safek Get: In This Case, Chalitza Does Not Send the Wrong Message Because Rachel Is Not Visibly in the Picture
Frequency of Cases: Divorce (Safek Get) Is Common; Building Collapse (Mapola) Is Rare; Therefore Stringencies Permissible Only Where Cost Is Low

topical_tags.term: chumra d'ati lidei kula (stringency leading to leniency)
